subject: [SO] Enable SO-Monitoring - use HTTPS and certInitializer
commit_message:
[SO] Enable SO-Monitoring - use HTTPS and certInitializer

- SO-Monitoring service exposed as NodePort
- Certs are retrieved dynamically using certInitializer

Issue-ID: SO-2920
Signed-off-by: Krzysztof Gajewski <krzysztof.gajewski@nokia.com>
Change-Id: I04e6556bcddc3c67afc2a76c5b4fecb59a134911

Krzysztof Gajewski

Hello,
first, you have an issue with secrets as said in comments

second, it doesn't seems to work as certificate is expired: https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//113072-7/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/nodeport_check_certs/certificates.html

(1 comment)

Sylvain, 
I'm afraid that's some SO-AAF cert exchange issue on testing environment. 
It's looks like so-monitoring consumes its certs which are pre installed inside jar, 
than loading files and config from /opt/app/osaaf/local directory. 
Nevertheless, initial so-monitoring logs denies that (https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//113072-7/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-so-monitoring-86c68f5b8b-6kq5m-so-monitoring-logs.html :

"JVM Arguments:  -Dspring.profiles.active=basic -Djava.security.egd=file:/dev/./urandom -Dlogs_dir=app/logs/ -Dlogging.config=/app/logback-spring.xml  -Djavax.net.ssl.trustStore=file:///opt/app/osaaf/local/org.onap.so.trust.jks -Djavax.net.ssl.trustStorePassword=Q!KICU6G+js[wB#c#y[ut&+l -Dspring.config.additional-location=/app/config/override.yaml " 

certs stored inside jar has another password)
JVM Arguments seem to be fine. Exact the same so-monitoring config seem to work correctly on our local environment.

I've compared some aaf config from pod with correct certs and maybe the reason is in aaf config:
i.e. sdc-fe
Types:           file,jks,pkcs12,script
Namespace:       org.onap.sdc

so-monitoring
Types:           pkcs12,script
Namespace:       org.onap.so
(https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//113072-7/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-sdc-fe-794899575b-mvznw-sdc-fe-aaf-config-logs.html ; 
https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//113072-7/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-so-monitoring-86c68f5b8b-6kq5m-so-monitoring-cert-init-aaf-config-logs.html )
but this seem to be out of my control.

Additional argument for that suspicion is info from gating about certs in other so components.
In my patchset so-vnfm-adapter is expired (similar config to mine), so -[SSL: WRONG_VERSION_NUMBER] 
And I had similar experience in our environment around patchset 1.

Are we able to verify that on testing environment?

code to the working patchset9/12

Patch Set 14:

> Patch Set 14: Code-Review-1
> 
> (2 comments)
> 
> 2 lines that I asked are still not removed. Plz fix this so that we can have password generated per dpeloyment

Hi

I wouldn't agree here with your proposal. This are SO Monitoring GUI credentials. With that approach you force user to get password form k8s secret and next login to GUI.
If we want to go with such approach then we need to do the same in Portal in SDC etc.

br,
Krzysztof Kuźmicki

Patch Set 14:

> Patch Set 14:
> 
> > Patch Set 14: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > (2 comments)
> > 
> > 2 lines that I asked are still not removed. Plz fix this so that we can have password generated per dpeloyment
> 
> Hi
> 
> I wouldn't agree here with your proposal. This are SO Monitoring GUI credentials. With that approach you force user to get password form k8s secret and next login to GUI.
> If we want to go with such approach then we need to do the same in Portal in SDC etc.
> 
> br,
> Krzysztof Kuźmicki

Well this is the ultimate target for the "no password harcoding" trend

We want users to be secure when they deploy ONAP with default configuration so all passwords that limit access to any onap external interfaces shouldn't be hardcoded.

Obviously if user wants to use those credentials there is no problem for him to set this in the override
